name: McLaren
bio: The reigning constructors' champions. McLaren's renaissance under Andrea Stella has been one of F1's great comeback stories — from mid-table mediocrity in the early 2020s to dominant champions. Papaya and carbon livery, British heritage, relentless development.
notable: Norris claimed the 2025 WDC, McLaren's first since Hamilton in 2008

name: Mercedes
bio: The most successful constructor of the hybrid era. After Hamilton's departure to Ferrari, Mercedes reloaded with Russell and rookie Antonelli — the Italian prodigy. Early 2026 results suggest the Silver Arrows are back in title contention.
notable: 8 consecutive constructors' titles 2014–2021. Antonelli leads the 2026 WDC

name: Ferrari
bio: The most storied team in F1 history. Hamilton's move to Maranello electrified the sport — but the marriage of F1's greatest driver with the sport's most passionate team comes with enormous expectation. Ferrari's 2026 regulation car is their most ambitious in years.
notable: Most championships in F1 history. Hamilton joined for 2025 in the most anticipated signing in decades

name: Red Bull Racing
bio: The team that Verstappen built into a dynasty. Red Bull's run of dominance was interrupted by McLaren in 2025, and 2026's regulation reset is their opportunity to rebuild. Hadjar joins as a promising young talent replacing the departed Tsunoda.
notable: Verstappen's 4 WDC titles (2021–24). Hadjar promoted from Racing Bulls

name: Aston Martin
bio: Lawrence Stroll's ambitious project to turn Aston Martin into a championship contender. The 2023 season showed genuine pace, but development has plateaued. Alonso remains their talisman — somehow still extracting extraordinary performances at 44.
notable: Alonso's 2023 run of podiums put Aston Martin back on the map. Still seeking their first win

name: Alpine
bio: The Renault-powered team operating under the Alpine brand. After losing Alonso and then struggling in the midfield, Alpine's 2026 project features Colapinto — Argentina's first F1 driver in 50 years — alongside the experienced Gasly. Renault's long-term commitment to F1 as an engine supplier continues.
notable: Colapinto's signing is the biggest story for South American F1 fans in decades

name: Williams
bio: The most successful independent constructor in F1 history, currently rebuilding under James Vowles. Sainz's arrival brought world-class credibility — his 2025 debut year included wins and pushed Williams up the order. Albon remains one of the grid's most underrated talents.
notable: 9 constructors' titles — but last in 1997. Sainz + Albon is their strongest lineup in decades

name: Haas F1 Team
bio: The American team with a Ferrari technical partnership. Haas's development model is unique — they outsource much of their car to Dallara while focusing on aero. Ocon and Bearman give them a genuinely competitive driver pairing for the first time in years.
notable: The only American constructor on the grid — until Cadillac arrived in 2026

name: Racing Bulls
full_name: Visa Cash App Racing Bulls F1 Team
bio: Red Bull's junior team, formerly Toro Rosso and then AlphaTauri. With Hadjar promoted to Red Bull, Racing Bulls enters 2026 with two of the most exciting young talents in F1 — Lawson's gritty experience and Lindblad's blinding raw pace. One to watch.
notable: Launch pad for Vettel, Ricciardo, Verstappen, and now the next generation

name: Audi F1 Team
bio: Formerly Sauber, now fully rebranded as Audi F1 Team as the German manufacturer begins its phased takeover. Hulkenberg is the experienced team leader; Bortoleto the future. 2026 is the beginning of Audi's proper F1 assault as they transition to their own power unit.
notable: Audi's entry into F1 as a manufacturer — one of the biggest stories of the 2026 season

name: Cadillac
bio: The 11th team on the 2026 grid and the first American constructor since the original Haas was founded. Cadillac/Andretti spent years fighting FOM for entry and finally arrived. Perez brings championship experience; Bottas brings veteran cool. A historic debut for American motorsport.
notable: First American constructor since Haas in 2016. Perez and Bottas: a wildly unexpected duo
